canonical_contract_assertions: Direct uploads may mutate only app.home_player_uploads and must prove teacher ownership of the exact media_asset_id before create.
Course-linked Home Player items are sourced from app.home_player_course_links and must remain governed lesson-media references.
GET /home/audio is the mounted learner-facing runtime surface and emits only the canonical media object { media_id, state, resolved_url } or null.
Runtime media is a read-only projection. It is never the source of inclusion truth and must not be written directly by Home Player flows.
Home Player playback is derived-audio only. Ready playback must resolve from a worker-produced MP3 derivative, never the source object.
Frontend is render-only for Home Player text. Exact copy for load, auth, processing, and failure surfaces is backend-owned.

bootstrap_gate.evidence: PowerShell bootstrap failed at ops/mcp_bootstrap_gate.ps1:113 because the returned object did not expose a headers property.
bootstrap_gate.impact: Bootstrap evidence is incomplete, but direct MCP connector retrieval remained available and was used for canonical table and storage verification.

runtime_projection_probe.result: ERROR: column purpose does not exist
runtime_projection_probe.meaning: Home Player purpose scoping cannot come from app.runtime_media. Source truth remains in app.home_player_uploads, app.home_player_course_links, and app.media_assets.

repo_truth_observations: backend/app/routes/studio.py currently validates purpose and media_type for direct uploads but does not prove current-teacher ownership of the asset before inserting app.home_player_uploads.

backend/app/services/home_audio_service.py already composes /home/audio through canonical source rows and a resolved media object, which narrows backend work to hardening authority and media lifecycle compliance rather than redesigning the route.
frontend/lib/features/studio/widgets/home_player_upload_routing.dart currently accepts WAV and MP3 but rejects M4A even though the canonical ingest contract allows it.
frontend/lib/features/studio/widgets/home_player_upload_dialog.dart currently keeps polling after generic status-refresh failures and does not stop immediately on 401 or 403 auth failures.
backend/app/services/studio_home_player_text_catalog.py contains encoding-corrupted Swedish strings, and the mounted learner home page does not yet consume /home/audio.

dependency_spine: HP-SEC-001 and HP-CONTRACT-002 are the independent blockers.
HP-SEC-001 + HP-CONTRACT-002 + HP-BE-003 + HP-FE-004 + HP-TEXT-005 + HP-UI-006 -> HP-VERIFY-007

tasks.title: Security and ownership guard
scope: Fail closed on direct-upload create so POST /studio/home-player/uploads can write app.home_player_uploads only after the caller proves ownership of the exact media_asset_id.
Keep ownership authority in canonical backend substrates and route guards rather than frontend payloads, storage paths, or mirrored teacher_id fields.
Preserve course-link ownership boundaries as separate doctrine so this task hardens direct uploads without reopening lesson-media or course-link authority.

contract_cross_checks: home_audio_aggregation_contract.md requires direct-upload create to prove teacher ownership of the exact media_asset_id and allows mutation only in app.home_player_uploads.
media_unified_authority_contract.md forbids frontend or projection layers from becoming ownership authority.
media_pipeline_contract.md keeps media_asset_id and asset lifecycle as the canonical ingestion boundary.
substrate_checks: Supabase connector verified app.home_player_uploads and app.media_assets exist.

Supabase connector verified storage.objects and storage.buckets exist, with course-media private and public-media public; ownership must therefore be enforced before any storage-derived access path is considered authoritative.
repo_findings: backend/app/routes/studio.py currently checks only purpose == home_player_audio and media_type == audio before inserting the upload row.
No current direct-upload create check proves the asset belongs to the current teacher.

expected_outcome: Direct-upload creation rejects foreign media_asset_id values even when the asset has the correct purpose and media_type.
Authorized teachers can still attach their own canonical home_player_audio assets without changing course-link doctrine.
Ownership truth for direct uploads is explicit, backend-owned, and test-covered.
verification_requirements: Add a backend regression that attempts to create a Home Player upload with a foreign media_asset_id and proves the write is rejected and app.home_player_uploads remains unchanged.
Add a positive-path backend regression that proves the owning teacher can create the row and that the stored media_asset_id matches the canonical asset.
Verify by search and test review that no alternate Home Player direct-upload create path bypasses the ownership guard.
tasks.go_condition: GO when every mounted Home Player direct-upload create path fails closed on non-owned assets and the only successful write path creates app.home_player_uploads rows for teacher-owned home_player_audio assets.
tasks.blocked_condition: BLOCKED if any direct-upload path still accepts purpose/media_type checks without ownership proof, or if ownership depends on storage path heuristics, mirrored teacher_id fields, or frontend claims.

tasks.title: Contract authority lock
scope: Lock the Home Player authority stack so all downstream work shares one accepted doctrine for direct uploads, course links, runtime projection, media lifecycle, text authority, and learner UI mounting.

Freeze canonical read and write surfaces: POST /api/home-player/media-assets/upload-url for ingest, GET /api/media-assets/{media_asset_id}/status for lifecycle polling, and GET /home/audio for learner runtime reads.

Freeze canonical response shape and forbidden payloads so no downstream task emits media_asset_id, signed URL, download URL, playback_object_path, or playback_format through the learner runtime contract.

contract_cross_checks: home_audio_aggregation_contract.md declares app.home_player_uploads and app.home_player_course_links as inclusion truth and requires mounted learner UI to consume GET /home/audio.
home_audio_runtime_contract.md limits learner payloads to media { media_id, state, resolved_url } or null and explicitly forbids media_asset_id and storage fields in the response.
media_unified_authority_contract.md defines app.runtime_media as read-only projection and bars direct writes.
media_pipeline_contract.md declares the canonical upload-url and status endpoints and requires worker-derived MP3 playback only.
system_text_authority_contract.md makes backend text authoritative for studio Home Player failures and learner /home/audio load or access failures.
substrate_checks: Supabase connector verified app.home_player_uploads, app.home_player_course_links, app.media_assets, and app.runtime_media all exist.
The failed purpose probe against app.runtime_media proves runtime_media cannot be treated as source truth for Home Player scope because it does not own a purpose discriminator.
repo_findings: backend/app/services/home_audio_service.py already emits the canonical media object shape for /home/audio.
frontend/lib/features/home/presentation/home_dashboard_page.dart is mounted but does not yet consume GET /home/audio, so the learner-facing contract remains unfulfilled.

expected_outcome: All downstream remediation tasks can rely on one non-competing doctrine for source truth, runtime projection, text authority, and learner mounting.
No task in this DAG is allowed to reopen write-surface, response-shape, or ownership decisions already ratified by the active contracts.
The Home Player remediation sequence becomes deterministic because target endpoints, fields, and non-authoritative layers are frozen before implementation work starts.
verification_requirements: Cross-check the five Home Player contracts against mounted backend and frontend surfaces and record any competing doctrine before work begins.
Search the repo for forbidden learner runtime fields such as media_asset_id, signed_url, download_url, playback_object_path, and playback_format within Home Player runtime consumers and producers.
Confirm that no task below this node assumes app.runtime_media is a writable source table or that learner UI may read any surface other than GET /home/audio.
tasks.go_condition: GO when the canonical Home Player write, status, runtime-read, media-shape, and text-authority boundaries are frozen and no competing active authority survives in contracts or mounted code.
tasks.blocked_condition: BLOCKED if any downstream plan still treats runtime_media as source truth, allows alternate learner runtime endpoints, or preserves forbidden runtime payload fields.

tasks.title: Backend compliance alignment
tasks.phase: PHASE 3 - BACKEND RUNTIME AND PIPELINE ALIGNMENT
scope: Align GET /home/audio, studio library reads, and Home Player media status handling to the accepted contracts without inventing new authority surfaces.

Enforce canonical media lifecycle rules: non-ready items may surface with media.resolved_url = null, terminal failed items remain failed, and ready items must resolve only through worker-derived MP3 playback.

Preserve runtime-media read-only projection compliance so backend composition consumes source truth plus canonical playback resolution rather than writing app.runtime_media or exposing raw storage fields.

contract_cross_checks: home_audio_aggregation_contract.md allows non-ready runtime items only when resolved_url is null and requires ready but unresolvable items to be excluded.
home_audio_runtime_contract.md defines the exact learner runtime media object and forbids storage-facing leakage.
media_pipeline_contract.md requires worker-derived MP3 playback and defines terminal failure and polling-stop semantics that backend status surfaces must preserve.
media_unified_authority_contract.md limits runtime_media to read projection duty.
substrate_checks: Supabase connector verified app.media_assets exposes media_type, purpose, ingest_format, playback_object_path, playback_format, state, and error_message.

Supabase connector verified app.runtime_media exposes only projection columns such as lesson_media_id, lesson_id, course_id, media_asset_id, media_type, playback_object_path, playback_format, and state.
The absence of a purpose column on app.runtime_media reinforces that purpose and inclusion truth must be derived from source tables plus app.media_assets.
repo_findings: backend/app/services/home_audio_service.py already resolves media through lesson_playback_service.resolve_media_asset_playback and omits invalid ready items.
Remaining backend work is to close ownership, lifecycle, and contract-consistency gaps rather than replace the entire /home/audio route.

expected_outcome: Every Home Player backend surface composes from canonical source rows, canonical media asset state, and canonical playback resolution only.
Ready Home Player audio resolves exclusively to a worker-derived MP3 object, while non-ready and failed states preserve the accepted visibility rules.
No learner or studio Home Player payload leaks raw asset/storage authority fields or writes runtime projection tables directly.
verification_requirements: Add or update backend tests proving that ready but unresolvable items are excluded, non-ready items return media.resolved_url = null, and failed items preserve terminal failure semantics.

Verify that GET /home/audio and studio Home Player library payloads emit only the canonical media object and do not emit media_asset_id, signed URLs, download URLs, playback_object_path, or playback_format.
Search backend/app for insert or update behavior against app.runtime_media in Home Player code paths and fail the node if any mounted write path remains.
tasks.go_condition: GO when mounted backend Home Player reads and status surfaces match the accepted runtime and media-pipeline contracts and no Home Player code writes runtime_media or exposes non-MP3 playback.
tasks.blocked_condition: BLOCKED if any Home Player backend surface still serves source audio, leaks forbidden runtime fields, writes app.runtime_media, or misclassifies failed or auth states.

tasks.title: Frontend parsing and polling fix
scope: Align studio Home Player upload routing to canonical audio ingest acceptance, including M4A support in addition to WAV and MP3.
Align status polling to canonical media lifecycle rules so polling begins only after upload completion succeeds and stops immediately on ready, failed, 401, or 403.
Keep the frontend parser render-only by consuming the canonical Home Player media object and refusing any dependency on raw asset or storage fields.

contract_cross_checks: media_pipeline_contract.md allows source ingest formats wav, mp3, and m4a and requires polling to stop on ready, failed, 401, and 403 while treating transient failures as non-state transitions.
home_audio_runtime_contract.md forbids frontend reliance on media_asset_id and storage-facing learner runtime fields.
system_text_authority_contract.md reserves exact failure and status text to the backend.
substrate_checks: Supabase connector verified app.media_assets is the canonical source of state and error_message for Home Player polling surfaces.
Storage buckets exist but remain non-authoritative to the frontend; the frontend must therefore consume backend status and runtime payloads rather than infer readiness from storage.
repo_findings: frontend/lib/features/studio/widgets/home_player_upload_dialog.dart currently leaves the polling timer alive after generic refresh failures and does not explicitly stop on 401 or 403.

expected_outcome: Studio upload UI accepts the full canonical audio ingest set and rejects non-audio input without inventing unsupported media types.
Studio polling behavior is deterministic and contract-compliant: stop on ready, failed, or auth failure; keep transient failures as non-terminal and optionally manual-refresh only.
Frontend Home Player parsing stays render-only and contract-safe.
verification_requirements: Add or update unit tests proving that M4A files are accepted by Home Player upload routing while unsupported non-audio files remain rejected.
Add or update widget tests proving that polling stops on ready, failed, 401, and 403 and that transient refresh failures do not keep mutating state indefinitely.
Search frontend Home Player studio code for forbidden dependencies on playback_object_path, playback_format, media_asset_id, signed_url, or download_url.
tasks.go_condition: GO when the mounted studio Home Player upload flow accepts the canonical ingest formats, obeys status polling stop rules, and consumes only backend-owned Home Player payloads.
tasks.blocked_condition: BLOCKED if M4A remains rejected, polling continues after auth or terminal failure, or frontend parsing depends on non-canonical media fields.

tasks.title: Text authority remediation
scope: Restore backend-owned exact copy for all Home Player studio and learner failure or status surfaces, including library load, upload start, upload save, upload refresh, auth, processing-terminal failure, course-link failure, and learner /home/audio load or access failure.
Repair encoding corruption in the Home Player backend text catalog so mounted Swedish copy is clean and deterministic.
Preserve DB-owned content boundaries so app.home_player_uploads.title and app.home_player_course_links.title remain domain content rather than text-catalog substitutions.

contract_cross_checks: system_text_authority_contract.md names Home Player studio failures and learner /home/audio failures as backend-owned exact-copy surfaces.
home_audio_aggregation_contract.md and home_audio_runtime_contract.md require mounted learner UI to consume backend-authored /home/audio output rather than frontend-authored fallback doctrine.
substrate_checks: app.home_player_uploads.title and app.home_player_course_links.title are DB-owned domain content and should not be replaced by text-catalog constants.
app.media_assets.error_message remains substrate-level evidence for terminal processing failure and must feed backend-owned text surfaces rather than frontend-authored copy.
repo_findings: backend/app/services/studio_home_player_text_catalog.py currently contains encoding-corrupted Home Player strings.
Existing frontend text-authority coverage is studio-focused and does not yet cover mounted learner /home/audio failure surfaces.

expected_outcome: Home Player exact copy is backend-owned across both studio and learner surfaces, with clean Swedish text and no frontend hardcoded fallback doctrine.
DB domain titles remain DB-owned content while backend-owned status and error text fields remain the only exact-copy authority for Home Player failures and processing state.
Text-authority coverage becomes complete enough for learner /home/audio UI integration to consume without inventing local copy.
verification_requirements: Add or extend tests proving that Home Player frontend files do not hardcode exact-copy error or status strings for covered studio and learner surfaces.
Verify that backend Home Player payloads expose backend_status_text or backend_error_text for all contract-required failure states.
Review the repaired backend text catalog to confirm encoding is clean and that DB-owned title fields were not folded into the text catalog.
tasks.go_condition: GO when all Home Player exact-copy status and failure surfaces are backend-owned, cleanly encoded, and consumed render-only by mounted frontend code.
tasks.blocked_condition: BLOCKED if mojibake survives, learner /home/audio failure copy is missing, or frontend code still authors exact failure/status text locally.

tasks.title: Learner /home/audio integration
scope: Mount a learner-facing Home Player section on the Home dashboard that consumes GET /home/audio and no alternate Home Player runtime source.
Consume the canonical runtime media object only, rendering audio-only playback from resolved_url when ready and preserving null media handling for non-ready states.
Integrate backend-owned text for load and access failure states and keep the learner UI render-only with respect to runtime authority.

contract_cross_checks: home_audio_aggregation_contract.md and home_audio_runtime_contract.md both require the mounted learner-facing Home Player UI to consume GET /home/audio.
home_audio_runtime_contract.md requires runtime playback to arrive only through the media object and forbids exposing asset or storage authority fields to the learner UI.
system_text_authority_contract.md requires backend-owned learner /home/audio load and access failure copy.
substrate_checks: Supabase connector verified the source substrates app.home_player_uploads and app.home_player_course_links exist, which confirms the learner UI must compose through backend runtime aggregation rather than direct table access.
Supabase connector verified app.runtime_media exists only as read projection support, reinforcing that learner UI must not bypass GET /home/audio.
repo_findings: frontend/lib/features/home/presentation/home_dashboard_page.dart currently renders only CoursesShowcaseSection and does not consume /home/audio.
frontend/test/widgets/home_dashboard_runtime_contract_test.dart currently encodes the old MVP-only home dashboard assumption.

expected_outcome: The mounted learner Home dashboard includes a Home Player section backed only by GET /home/audio.
Learner playback remains audio-only, contract-safe, and render-only, with ready and non-ready states rendered according to the canonical media object.
Learner failure and access messaging comes from backend-owned text fields rather than local fallback copy.
verification_requirements: Add or update widget tests proving that the mounted home dashboard requests GET /home/audio and renders canonical Home Player items from the returned media object.
Add repository or controller tests proving that non-ready items preserve null media handling and ready items use resolved_url without expecting forbidden fields.
Search mounted learner Home Player UI code to confirm there is no alternate endpoint, direct storage access, or direct runtime_media access.
tasks.go_condition: GO when the mounted learner Home dashboard consumes GET /home/audio, renders only canonical audio runtime state, and uses backend-owned text for Home Player failures.
tasks.blocked_condition: BLOCKED if the learner home page still omits Home Player, consumes any alternate source, or relies on raw asset/storage/runtime projection fields.

tasks.title: Final contract verification
scope: Run the aggregate stop-go verification for Home Player across ownership authority, contract compliance, backend runtime behavior, studio polling, text authority, learner mounting, and substrate truth.
Re-run MCP connector checks for canonical table existence, storage bucket visibility, and runtime-media projection shape after the remediation closes.
Fail the DAG if any prior node leaves an unresolved competing authority, forbidden runtime field, non-MP3 playback leak, or unmounted learner /home/audio surface.

contract_cross_checks: All five Home Player contracts must agree with the mounted repo state and with the verification outputs gathered under this node.
Any surviving contradiction between ownership, runtime payload shape, media lifecycle, frontend text authority, or learner mounting is a hard failure.
substrate_checks: Re-run MCP connector verification for app.home_player_uploads, app.home_player_course_links, app.media_assets, app.runtime_media, storage.objects, and storage.buckets.
Re-run the runtime projection probe that previously failed on purpose to confirm no implementation silently moved Home Player scope ownership into app.runtime_media.
Reconfirm storage bucket visibility assumptions so no remediation task accidentally made source or playback storage public beyond contract.
repo_findings: This node depends on all prior nodes because the Home Player contracts span backend, frontend, storage, and text authority simultaneously.
The latest no-code audit DAG requires a single final stop-go pass rather than independent green claims from each remediation slice.

expected_outcome: A final deterministic verification record proves Home Player is contract-compliant across ownership, media ingestion and playback, runtime projection, frontend polling, backend text authority, and learner mounting.
The Home Player remediation DAG can be closed without unresolved contradictions or missing substrate evidence.
Any remaining mismatch is surfaced as a hard blocker with the exact node that failed.
verification_requirements: Run the targeted backend and frontend Home Player test suites required by prior nodes and record pass or fail against this manifest.
Search the mounted repo for forbidden Home Player learner runtime fields and for any direct write to app.runtime_media.
Re-run MCP connector substrate checks and fail verification if canonical tables, buckets, or read-only projection assumptions drift from the accepted contracts.
tasks.go_condition: GO when every prior Home Player remediation node is closed, all required tests and substrate checks pass, and no conflicting authority survives in code, contracts, or storage posture.
tasks.blocked_condition: BLOCKED if any prior node remains open, any substrate check drifts, learner mounting is incomplete, or forbidden Home Player authority or payload patterns remain.
